name: x402-spendguard
description: A spend firewall for AI agents making x402 payments. Enforces caps, allowlists, signature-integrity binding, and a kill switch before any payment is signed. Pre-alpha; v1 is anti-drain + signature integrity, EVM-only.
author: Kevin Brown <x402.spendguard@gmail.com>
//exports: The barrel is the SOLE public path (PKG-02/04). No internal subpath and no wildcard, so a deep import throws ERR_PACKAGE_PATH_NOT_EXPORTED at runtime AND cannot type-resolve under nodenext — the 'sole path by construction' discipline of the signer wrap, applied to the package boundary.
//files: ALLOWLIST what a published tarball ships (allowlist > denylist — same lesson as the signer route-closure). dist ONLY: compiled JS + declarations, NO source and NO maps (PKG-03/05). This keeps the whole test tree (test/e2e imports node:http + fetch) and raw source OUT of the artifact, so 'no egress' holds for what a stranger installs, not just for src/. README/LICENSE/package.json are added by npm itself.
